items.title: Lectric’s soon-to-end Memorial Day e-bike sale with up to $720 savings lasts through the month – prices from $799
items.summary:
As part of the final days of the Lectric Memorial Day Sale running through the rest of the weekend and the month, we’re shining a spotlight on the brand’s XP Trike2 750 Long-Range eTrikes with a FREE $558 bundle at $1,799 shipped right now, while the standard XP Trike2 models are getting FREE $455 bundles at $1,499 shipped. Were it not for the discounts on the bundled gear, these packages would run you $2,357 and $1,954 in full, which gives you one of the largest savings to date on these e-trikes. The standard model bundles include front and rear steel-encased baskets, a suspension seat post, an Elite headlight, and a support seat, while the XP Trike2 750 bundles add in a pair of mirrors and an accordion-style bike lock. You can also browse the full lineup of other e-bike deals down below – plus, the brand is offering up to 30% Anniversary savings on a wide array of extra batteries starting from $210, as well as the ongoing pre-order deals for its new XPress2 Cruiser and Commuter e-bikes with $336 bundles at $1,399.

items.title: Lectric celebrates 7th Anniversary with rare 30% savings on extra batteries for new + legacy e-bikes at lows starting from $210

Within the lineup, you’ll find batteries for various new and legacy models – even those no longer sold from the brand – rounded together below, and prices are ranging from $210 shipped to $490 shipped. We usually see occasional flash sales throughout each year with 15% to 20% discounts, though this sale is mirroring the greater 30% discounts we usually only see during Black Friday and giving folks a chance at their best prices. Through the rest of the weekend (and month), you can save up to $210 on batteries for the latest e-bikes, as well as for legacy models, like the XP Lite1, XP Lite2, XP 3, XPeak1, and XPedition1. Head below to browse the full lineup, and be sure to also check out the full Memorial Day e-bike lineup in our original coverage here.
